title: LORE: A Large Generative Model for Search Relevance
abstract: LORE is a complete, replicable blueprint for LLM-based relevance modeling that spans the entire lifecycle. The paper presents a two-stage training paradigm: first using progressive CoT synthesis and Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) to instill comprehensive capabilities, then applying Reinforcement Learning (RL) to align the model with human preferences. The authors constructed a benchmark called RAIR for evaluation and designed a query-based stratified deployment strategy for real-time computation.
key_insights: Naive teacher CoT distillation results in negative effects
Long CoT is not necessary for better performance
Discussion on Multimodal Modeling: VLM or Two-Stage LLM?
applications: Practical solution for developing advanced e-commerce relevance systems
benchmarks: RAIR: A comprehensive benchmark tailored to evaluate core capabilities
deployment_strategy: Query-based stratified deployment strategy for real-time computation
